mode_chosen_by: rule-2 cumulative: branch is 2 commits ahead of main, working tree clean, no fresh cumulative-mode record for current HEAD

findings.summary: Test evidence is stale/missing (prawduct-hook test-status exit 1: no .test-evidence.json). Cannot confirm the suite passes from this session. Builder must run ./scripts/test.sh and capture evidence before merge. Repo is not Prawduct-onboarded (no .prawduct/project-state.yaml) so the gate may not be wired.

Vector encode/decode is duplicated: collection_factory._vector_to_text/_vector_from_text reimplement schema_backed._encode_vector/_decode_vector. collection_factory already imports _require_pgvector from schema_backed, so the seam exists. The two pairs also diverge subtly (_encode_vector json.dumps -> '[0.1, 0.2]' with spaces vs _vector_to_text -> '[0.1,0.2]'; _decode_vector guards non-list/non-str JSON, _vector_from_text does not). Extract one shared pair to keep the two pgvector text contracts from drifting.
import json is inline inside _vector_from_text (collection_factory.py:72) and is the only json use in the module; a module-level import is more idiomatic. Cosmetic.

summary: 2 warnings, 1 note. No BLOCKING findings. Vector + L2-wiring + asyncpg-Record fixes (#85/#86) are well-targeted with strong code-analyzed coverage (unit + real-driver integration test reproducing the exact bugs). SQLite additive-initialize and pk-serialization refactors are correct (reset() clears anchor/initialized/schema_info, so re-init composes). PR-gate-wise: no unresolved blockers. Resolve the stale test evidence before merge and consider deduplicating the vector codec.
